rule: Use the EER/workload analytical matrix to activate a query-specific family.

real_artifact_examples: IMDb QG9: $G0,G3,G6,G7$; FIBEN Q2: $Control,G3,G7$; LDBC IC7: $G0,G3,G6$
rule: Preserve candidates that keep entities external or reference-oriented.
purpose: Tests whether reference-preserving designs alone are sufficient.

real_artifact_examples: IMDb QG9: $G2,G4,G5,G8,G9$; FIBEN Q2: $G5,G9$; LDBC IC7: $G4$
rule: Preserve candidates that materialise related data under, or close to, the selected root.
purpose: Tests whether locality-oriented embedding is sufficient.

real_artifact_examples: IMDb QG9: $G2,G8,G9$; FIBEN Q2: $G9$; LDBC IC7: $G3,G4,G6$
rule: Select candidates using structural depth without relationship semantics, sharedness, or update-volatility evidence.
purpose: Tests whether structural coverage alone explains the winners.

real_artifact_examples: IMDb QG9: $G0,G2,G3$; FIBEN Q2: $G3$; LDBC IC7: $G0,G3$
rule: Use the dominant relationship type without the full analytical matrix.
purpose: Tests whether semantic family alone is enough.

rule: Sample the same number of candidates as SchemaLens for the same query-scale case.
purpose: Non-explanatory sanity check for reduced-space size.
